mcq.explanation_md: In a row-direction flex container, the **main axis** runs horizontally, and `justify-content` distributes items along the main axis — so `justify-content: center` centers them left-to-right. `align-items: center` is the tempting twin: it works on the **cross axis**, which in a row means *vertical* centering. The pair only makes sense together with direction: flip to `flex-direction: column` and their roles swap. `text-align` centers inline text inside an element, not flex items, and `margin: auto` on the container centers the container itself, not its children.
